title: Greetings & Goodbyes
shortSummary: Greet people, introduce yourself, and say goodbye in Italian, the foundation of every conversation.
culturalNote: Italians greet warmly and often. Ciao is the friendly hello and goodbye, but only with people you know; with strangers, elders, or in shops use the more respectful buongiorno (good day) or buonasera (good evening). A handshake suits first meetings, while friends and family exchange a light kiss on each cheek. The key social choice is tu versus Lei: use the informal tu with friends and peers, and the formal Lei (capitalised in writing) with people you have just met or in professional settings until you are invited to switch.

grammar.explanation: Italian has two ways to say 'you': tu (informal) and Lei (formal). Use tu with friends, family, and children; use Lei with strangers, elders, and at work. Lei takes the lui/lei verb form.
grammar.commonMistake: Using tu with someone you just met, which can sound too familiar.

grammar.explanation: Use buongiorno during the day and buonasera in the evening. Buonanotte (good night) is only for going to bed, not a general evening greeting.
grammar.commonMistake: Saying buonanotte when you arrive in the evening; it only means 'good night' before sleep.

dialogue.setup: Signora Bianchi meets her new colleague Signor Rossi in the morning.

lines.speaker: Sig. Rossi
lines.native: Buongiorno! Molto bene, grazie. E Lei?
lines.english: Good morning! Very well, thank you. And you?

lines.speaker: Sig.ra Bianchi
lines.native: Sto bene. Sono la signora Bianchi.

lines.speaker: Sig. Rossi
lines.english: Nice to meet you! My name is Paolo Rossi.

lines.speaker: Sig.ra Bianchi
lines.english: Nice to meet you. Welcome to the team!

lines.speaker: Sig. Rossi
lines.english: Thank you very much. See you soon!

lines.speaker: Sig.ra Bianchi
lines.english: See you soon. Have a nice day!

quiz.question: How do you say 'Good evening' in Italian?
quiz.explanation: Buonasera is used from the late afternoon onward.

quiz.question: Which greeting is formal?
quiz.explanation: It uses the formal Lei; the others are casual.

quiz.question: Come _____? (informal 'how are you')
quiz.explanation: With tu, the verb stare becomes stai.

quiz.question: Which word means a casual 'hi' or 'bye'?
quiz.explanation: Ciao is the informal greeting and farewell.

quiz.question: Arrange: bene / sto / grazie
quiz.explanation: Sto bene grazie means 'I'm well, thank you'.
